author.name: The world is always ending, relax.

entry.title: The_medium_you_learn_from_is_important
entry.content:
Table of Contents

  1. The medium you learn from is no neutral
  2. Cost of production
  3. Types of consumers
  4. Snopes fact check (tm)
  5. How you engage
  6. What to do

The medium you learn from is no neutral

An increasing trend of people learning from youtube videos is causing great harm to people (5bathrooms) As people seek to become smarterer they will increasing fall into the same traps, I’m going to talk about two mediums (books and youtube) this isn’t to say one is better than the other because I don’t believe one is, if you find books incredibly boring your time would be better spent learning from something else, I’m doing this to illustrate concepts that can be applied elsewhere

Cost of production

If I want to make a book up until very recently (chatgpt) it was costly in every sense of the word, it would take a lot of time, a lot of effort and would have a high chance of failure since self publishing is still not incredibly viable especially for education based books - not only that but in every education based book publishers care about the author thats why they will credential dump and give you a history of the author that serves to make them seem credible - for a youtube video almost none of this is the case, you can make a youtube video about any topic in a matter of hours.

Types of consumers

This point is short and sweet, if a book becomes successful it gets more people reviewing and fact checking it, with youtube videos this is often not the case because of (a) culture and (b) the amount of youtube videos not only being made but the amount of youtube videos being made and going viral makes it so that there isn’t enough reviewers to actually address it.

Snopes fact check (tm)

Ask yourself, if you’re learning something by default you have to be watching content about things you don’t know, and if you don’t know fact checking them becomes incredibly difficult - If you have to fact check everything the amount of effort and time learning takes increases drastically.

I’m not saying never fact check I’m saying not being able to trust any of the information is much more common on a low cost video that’s meant ot fit a mass production scheme, think about how many education channels can somehow make educational videos on every single topic, it would have you believe that polymaths are a dime a dozen, whats much more likely is you can just spew a lot of misinfo on youtube without it mattering.

“oh but the comments will correct it” this isn’t true at all, someone who’s educationed on topic x and someone who’s learning about topic x are not watching the same videos, the overlap doesn’t exist. So now you’re back to having to double check any and all major claims, 2xing if not more the amount of time and effort it will take.

How you engage

Books require active engagement, videos don’t, I cannot read a book whilst playing video games, I can watch educational youtube videos, but if you’re doing this you should consider it entertainment and not learning, learning is deliberate, intentional and planned out ahead of time, it isn’t passively listening to words as you play league of legends. Each medium will encourage and discourage types of engagement but you can choose to watch the youtube video on 1x speed, fullscreen whilst writing notes.

What to do

Heres the part where I mostly shill for institutions because they’re checking who produces the content, they’re also held to a much higher level of scrutiny, MIT, Stanford and even harvard upload lectures to youtube. In summary

  1. Who’s making the content?
  2. Are they an expert or generalist?
  3. What checks exist to make sure they’re accurate?
  4. What consequences do they face if they’re wrong?
  5. Are they incentivised to get views or educate people?
  6. Look for something they’ve made on a topic you know a lot about then critically review it

author.name: The world is always ending, relax.

entry.title: Pessimistic_people_will_kill_you
entry.content:
Pessimistic people will kill you

Misery loves company.

This statement is not often focused on enough, though it should be not just because it’s true but because it hints at the contagious forces of pessimism, there are some people who’re not only consistently pessimistic but actually derive a sense of security from others being pessimistic, they should be avoided at all costs because you simply not wanting to be pessimistic is not enough to guard you from them, you need to isolate them from you otherwise little by little you’ll become more pessimistic because pessimistic people outnumber you. They will spin any positive into a negative and soon your brain will start doing the same, you tell them they’ve found a cure for cancer and their first response is “but most popel won’t be able to afford it” - it’s also worth noting that pessimisism and a negative outlook seems to correlate with lower intelligence and cognitive function, it also seems causitive so if someoen is pessmistic they’re more likely to be stupid and if you become more pessimistic you’ll likely become stupider - Optimism simple requires more resources and higher levels of cognition and your brain is use it or lose it.

author.name: The world is always ending, relax.

entry.title: Dynamic_wake_times
entry.content:
Table of Contents

  1. Dynamic wake times
    1. Issues with sleep
    2. wake/sleep time as regulators
    3. Common sense
  2. My approach

Dynamic wake times

Issues with sleep

I’ve longstanding had issues with my sleep, struggling to sleep, sleeping too little or of insufficiant quality, meaning I’ve tried a lot of things and this is the only thing that has worked consistently overtime - it is not perfect and my sleep is still worse than I would like it to be as detailed in other blog posts like I dont sleep but this system still offers practical value in both it’s simplicity (set an alarm to go to sleep but not to wake up) and it’s application (can be done even when you have to wake up at a certain time range)

wake/sleep time as regulators

I want ot quickly note that our sleep wake cycles are regulated by a lot of things, food, social contact, blue light, the media we consume, when we exercise etc, but it’s more strongly set by going to sleep at the same time each day compared to waking up at the same time each day.

Common sense

If we stop and apply common sense to the idea of waking up at the same time each day it quickly falls apart - The ammount of sleep we need obviously varies drastically but the time we get tired doesn’t correlate with that, if I normally wake up at 8am and I normally need 8 hours of sleep, if one day I need 9 hours I might not know that I need 9 hours and more importantly that doesn’t mean I’d get tired at 11pm instead of midnight so on that day I’d likely get 8 hours when I need nine - or I could get 8 hours when I only needed 7.

My approach

My personal approach is to figure out what range of sleep I need, I need a maximum of 9 hours (though most days much less) so if I want to wake up at 8am I need to make sure each night I’m asleep by 11pm then whatever time I wake up at I get up at now when I say “wake up” I don’t mean it exactly if I wake up at 2am to piss I’m not going to stay up I’m going to go back to sleep, I mean times in which you wake up naturally - the aclimation is strange but in the long run it seems much better, I’ve had several nights where I’ve naturally woke up after 4-5 hours of sleep and been perfectly fine the next day - now when I say fine I mean relative to my historic trends not by the standards of medical professionals

This makes much more intuitive sense since the amount of sleep we need we will naturally try to achieve once asleep (i.e days where you ignore your alarm) and usually when we need more sleep we don’t go to bed earlier we just want ot wake up later - it actually gives you body a chance to self regulate - I’d also guess on some level your body need to be asleep to know how much sleep you need since how much sleep you need would also vary based on quality and quality is unknown until you actually are sleeping.

author.name: The world is always ending, relax.

entry.title: Slow_drip_poison
entry.content:
Slow drip poison

Socialisation is a foundational part of what it means to be human, it is non negotiable - community is one of the only things outside of survival we can say with certainty matters, people who self ascribe as introverts are engaging in a form of self harm - they’re otherising themselves from prosocial people, each person has a different level of socialisation they wish to engage in but this should never result in distinct and exclusionary catagories - do not define yourself by the fact other people want to socialise more or less than you or you’ll enter into an infinite fracturing a self fufilling profecy that leads to self destruction - to a lack of humanity. I preface this to avoid this getting misconstrued as me being an introvert or anti social. I am very prosocial and extroverted.

I want to document my experiance of socialisation, it should first be said since I live in a poor area socialisation is mostly isolated to drinking in the pub, I do not drink or do drugs but I still enjoy pubs and clubs, I enjoy seeing and talking to my friends, meeting new people and the small talk that comes with it. I feel energised, invigorated as though every single synapse in my brain is firing, my brain is suddenly switched on, thinking becomes effortless as does enjoying myself.

This typically continues until the early morning, as it comes to a close I feel an anxiety looming, a sadness building because I know what comes next, it comes after any social event, the dread, the loneliness, the feeling of despair, hopelessness - it always comes crashing onto me like a tidal waves washing me away from the people I care about stranding me to isolation.

For this reason I always opt to walk home alone, rain, cold it doesn’t make a difference I don’t want to be around anyone I don’t want to spend 20 minutes sat in an uber even if it’s with a stranger I don’t want to be in anyones proximity I just want to cease, to vanish, to be allowed to dissolve into nothingness. Each time I walk home I say I will feel better in the morning but it’s never the case, I feel progressively worse physically and mentally for the following days.

This is even seen in my biometrics, I can’t sleep and when I do I have fragmented vivid dreams that make me feel even worse than not sleeping at all, my HRV drops for several days, my resting heart rate increases for several days and I get almost like flu symptoms, a headache that doesn’t pass, an increase in my bodies temperature, as though my immune system is trying to fight my emotional state which is one of depression.

I will spend the next few days barely able to catch a thought, no control over my mind, a restlessness that makes me pace until my legs are sore, an inability to do anything, I can’t think, I can’t focus, I can’t sleep, I can’t enjoy anything, food tastes bland, nothing has color and my eyes can’t focus on anything. There is no relief you just have to wait, it’ll pass then the need to socialise builds until the cycle repeats.

author.name: The world is always ending, relax.

entry.title: I_wanna_be_software
entry.content:
I wanna be software

I’ve been a enslaved by death for as long as I’ve had thoughts, all consuming, all encompassing, all devouring - this will continue until my last breath. I’ve been in close proximity to death enough to keep it with me at all times. From the moment of our first awareness of death we conciously or unconciously carry that guilt with us, the guilt that we will die. That we will be a lasting harm to all around us, why should we not feel guilty about dying? the loss of someone you love is one of the strongest losses you can feel - ignoring the practical side of things we’re causing a great harm by dying.

From this moment the anxiety grows as does the resentment from knowing others will die - it’s a sin all will commit and none can avoid commiting but it’s still a sin for which we all must do our best to atone - this atonement comes in fufilling our roles past what is expected or asked, it comes from offering ourselves as permenance to continue after we can’t, it comes from understanding we are nothing beyond our relation to others, their wellbeing takes precident over our own. Do not allow yourself to be trapped by a gut response of thinking I’m suggesting you run yourself into the ground for others sake, a short and sickly life is not whats best for others, being as healthy as you can be and sustaining your value to others over time is the best for them, self destruction for a noble cause is idiocy, a mind virus that should be avoided.

Death comes in several stages, first there is the absence - a strange feeling almost as though in a deja vu induced confusion, an expectation continually going unmet, waiting for something to click into place and for you to feel normal again, like reaching for and then grasping a word you’ve forgotten - there isn’t any grasping however, just absence again and again like a sort of deja vu induced confusion and all you can do is endure it as you become a prisoner to your own mind, you don’t feel bad just off as if you’re stuck in a never ending cycle of deja vu induced confusion day after day, week after week. it seems like it’s infinitely repeating a never ending cycle though eventually it does end.

Now you see death clearly, the horror, the permenance, the unavoidability you know it still has more for you, that it will always have more for you - the fear permiates into you, the dread and anxiety seep out of your pores as though your skin is filled with a thick mucus - you try to speak but there isn’t any noise, just a low hum as you try your hardest at expression, but what you feel cannot be expressed, you’ve spent every waking minute trying to avoid it trying not to think about it and now you’re shackled by it, every thought in relation to it - you scream and everybody hears but you can’t even tell if you made a sound. Deafened by it you scream for as long as you can, until your voice gives out, until you run out of words then others begin to notice you’re no longer speaking - just noise, a noise you yourself cannot hear, they try to communicate but it isn’t there, the part of you that was human and communicates has long been exhausted, if death offered you some rest from it you would come back, be able to talk, be able to hear, be able to see - but it doesn’t let up, not because it wants you to suffer but because that is it’s nature, it cannot let up until transformation occurs, this is the only freedom - any other your mind will remain in shackles.

You now reach the stage of guilt, you’ve mostly put death out of your concious mind but you’re inconveinianced by it, a role that was being fufilled is now vacant - a friend, a partner, a relative, a person to confide in. Their death is now about you - the harm from them dying is now because they can no longer serve you, this selfishness we all exibit eventually - we’re nothing if not self serving, the fork in the road presents itself to you now, you know the harm from death continues on forever because a role is unmet.

The suffering you feel, experiance and go through, the harm of death comes from impermanence, you should try to free others from this as best you can, you should seek to make yourself redundant, uneeded - not because you’re bad at your role but because you’ve made your role have alternatives for fufillment - how else can you overcome the guilt? all you are is your capacity to help others and your memories, be as open to others as you can be so your memories and experiances aren’t lost and fufil your role so well that other people can fufil it for themselves, make the be able to take refuge in your even after you’ve died, continue to help them even after you’re dead, allow them to bring you back to life whenever they need you, make it so that they bring you back to life without even realising that’s what they’ve done. The selfishness is not worth the guilt, put them above yourself.

author.name: The world is always ending, relax.

entry.title: A_k_acc_manifesto
entry.content:
Table of Contents

  1. a k/acc manifesto
    1. Accelerationism
    2. Criticism of (x)/acc
    3. Accelerationism as natural process
    4. What is k/acc?
    5. Why k/acc has to be the first step
    6. Directive of k/acc

a k/acc manifesto

Accelerationism

Accelerationism is seen as either a hateful, aggressive militant ideology or as a natural forming process - as complexity increases, capacity follows then it’s only natural that things will get quicker. Some argue acc started with marx others argue it started with land I’m of the view marx was the origin and land was the form (though nick land should no longer be taken seriously, he now just boomer posts on twitter, at this point it’s not even worth reading his work directly since it’s explained in other works in a much more accessable form) Marx saw acc as a function of capitalism that could be stopped, overcame. Land saw it as unstoppable so embracing it is all that’s left - since then people have expanded it further - the most fitting analogy is that of a train, we’re sat on a train marxs looks out the window and notices it’s speeding up with no chance of stopping, he looks further down the track and sees the tracks ending, he wants the train to be slowed down and hopefully stopped - land looks out of the window and reaches the conclusion the train will only ever speed up so we should try and speed it up as much as possible to get it over and done with.

Extended from this is the idea of “clearing the pit” the train gets so much speed it’s existing momentum at the moment of derailing might keep us going until we either land on another track or until we can build one.

One common myth is that accelerationism is right leaning, this was not true of marx and (kind of) wasn’t true of nick land or the CCRU - included in accelerationism is l/acc seeking to reach automated luxery communism, g/acc seeking to abolish the concept of gender, xenofeminism seeking to use capitalism and acc to remove gender inequality to establish true feminism. CCRU was a very left leaning group and most if not all excluding land went on and continued to be incredibly impactful left leaning thinkers, two recognisable cases being sadie plant and mark fisher - claiming acc as right leaning wipes out all of the wokr done and being done by left leaning thinkers - just because right leaning people dominate acc spaces on twitter doesn’t mean they are the ideology, as is mostly the left has allowed the right to claim ownership over some thing that should rightfully belong to the left.

This should hopefully give enough of a background into acc that the rest logically follows.

Criticism of (x)/acc

The issues with most acc subgenres is they lack coherence, formal value sets and any actual structure - they mostly fall into the catagory of thing/acc with no actual considerations A logical prerequesit (that I’m currently violating) should be to write an outline of your thing/acc before you can put it in your twitter name or bio - watch almost any interview with accelerationists and you’ll see quickly how they’ve never thought about what it actually means.

Beff explains eacc to lex

It’s obvious from this clip that beff could explain it in simple language but chooses not to, I agree with feynman if you can’t explain it to a layman you don’t have a good enough grasp on it - watch that entire clip then ask what should an e/accer actually do? it’s laid out a value set but not a formal structure, an interesting note on that vidoe is beff claims d/acc as a fork to e/acc but I don’t know why he beleives this from the opening lines of the d/acc manifesto he explains it’s meant as a response to andreessens techno-optimist manifesto which makes me question if beff has even read it, e/acc is barely mentioned - but this isn’t meant to be focused too much on beff but unfortunately he’s the central figure and most notable in acc spaces right now even though I honest to god do not know if he’s doing anything beyond posting on twitter, he talks like he’s incredibly important but I’ve yet see anyone point to a solid thing he’s done, he’s talked about how vital open source and working in public is to progress but is incredibly evasive about the things he’s doing/working on - I’m not making the claim he’s a liar or a scammer moreso just if it’s impossible to verify if someones doing something it’s concerning - from his use of language in the clip he is knowlegable about accelerationism and seemingly the ideas of deluze and the other one but that’s what makes it even more frustrating right now (x)/acc is going through such a level of decentralisation that it’s fracturing and now the power is distributed among too many nodes and it’s potential for power growth is highly limited due to people aprehension of AI - meanwhile tenents of k/acc continue to grow in the mainstream.

Accelerationism as natural process

Look at human history and what you see is clear acceleration in all things, humans strive this striving causes creation and from creation comes complexity as complexity increases so does the potential for creation resulting in further complexity this process continues until the blissful or bitter end. But from complexity also comes a much faster rate of progression, of societal values, technology, human understanding, everything. it all requires a threshold level of complexity - this isn’t to say all forms of complexity are important and valuble, organic, inorganic and artifical all exist.

This cycle is not just a characteristic of human ambition but a reflection of the fundamental laws of nature and evolution. Just as biological organisms evolve by adapting to their environments in increasingly complex ways, so too does human society evolve by creating and adapting to new technologies and ideas. This evolution is not linear but accelerates as each layer of complexity adds depth and speed to the process. The result is a world where changes that once took centuries now unfold in decades, if not years, reshaping our societies and selves at an unprecedented pace. Through this lens, accelerationism can be seen not merely as a socio-political theory but as an extension of the natural process of evolution, magnified by human consciousness and creativity.

The acceleration of this process could lead to a tipping point where human society either adapts to its rapid pace of change or faces significant upheaval. This is where the concept of accelerationism becomes not just an observation but a critical framework for understanding and navigating the future. By embracing and accelerating these changes, rather than resisting them, humanity can harness this natural process for transformation. Through conscious effort and foresight, we have the potential to guide this acceleration in ways that promote sustainability, equality and well-being, seeing complexity not as a challenge but as an opportunity for unprecedented growth and advancement.

In essence, the process of complexity highlights the interconnectedness of human innovation with the evolutionary forces of nature. It acknowledges that while the path forward may contain challenges, the same processes that drive complexity and change also hold the keys to our adaptation and flourishing in an ever-accelerating world. Things will get faster regardless of human action - humans just seemingly have the largest capacity to make things go faster but everything from a whale to an ant is making things go faster.

As a small note here I want to point out complexity / complicatedness, the design, manufacture and shipping of a toothbrush is highly complex, it’s also not complicated.

What is k/acc?

k/acc or knowledge accelerationsim is a fundementally human centric approach to accelerationism - unless it furthers the goal of humans it is not included, an AI that irons clothes is disregarded, an AI that helps people learn is encouraged. k/acc is the belief that human conciousness is both unique and something we cannot replicate (though with sufficiant advancement we may come close), that a core part of that conciousness stems from knowledge and that knowledge in and of itself offers value in all aspects of life - it enables control over yourself, allows the world to be more interesting and almost every issue our species have faced and will face comes down to knowledge (or a lack of). Conflict and a lack of cooperation are no exception - without sufficiant knowledge nothing can be resolved. We are no more than an animal taking rough guesses based on memory and feelings - This has a hard limit on potential and our species overcoming this limit through knowledge is why we’ve come so much further.

Underpinning k/acc is the conviction that technological advancements must pivot around enhancing human capabilities and consciousness. Technology should not merely aim to automate tasks but to augment human intellect and creativity, bridging the gap between what is and what could be. In this light, k/acc champions technologies and innovations that empower individuals to navigate, understand, and interact with the world in more profound and impactful ways. The emphasis is on tools that facilitate learning, comprehension, and the application of knowledge in solving real-world problems, thereby enriching human experience and potential. Things like intellectual property and copyright take a backseat to enhancing the ability for people to actualise knowledge (art being a form of applied knowledge).

Central to k/acc is the idea that knowledge is not just power but the very foundation of progress and enlightenment. It is through the acquisition and application of knowledge that humans have the potential to be human. By fostering an environment where knowledge is freely accessed, shared, and applied, k/acc aims to catalyze a global movement towards a more human world. It is a call to action for all of humanity to embrace the pursuit of knowledge, not just as a means to an end, but as an end in itself.

In essence, k/acc serves as a manifesto for a future where technology and human intellect converge in a harmonious pursuit of progress and self-actualization. It is a fever dream vision of a world where every individual is empowered to be.

Why k/acc has to be the first step

The imperative that k/acc, be the initial step in the broader accelerationist movement stems from its foundational belief that knowledge underpins all forms of human progress and societal development. This conviction is not just philosophical but practical; before any technological, social, or political acceleration can be effectively pursued, a comprehensive and deeply ingrained understanding of knowledge as both a resource and a capability must be established.

Knowledge equips individuals and societies with the ability to make informed decisions. Without a profound and widespread understanding of the world, its complexities, and the potential consequences of our actions, attempts to accelerate in any domain are likely to be misguided, inefficient, or even harmful. True innovation requires a rich substrate of knowledge from which to draw. By prioritizing k/acc, we ensure that creativity and invention are not stifled by ignorance or limited perspectives. A society well-versed in a broad range of disciplines is better equipped to generate breakthroughs that can propel humanity forward in a meaningful way.

The acceleration of technology and societal change brings with it a host of ethical dilemmas and moral considerations. This will hamper widespread adoption k/acc allows you to push accelerationism without ever sayign the word.

Knowledge accelerationism fosters a sense of community and shared purpose. When individuals recognize the value of knowledge and its role in shaping the future, they are more likely to collaborate and contribute to collective endeavors. This unity is essential for the sustained, coordinated action required to navigate the challenges and opportunities of acceleration.

Directive of k/acc

The primary directive of k/acc, is to actively pursue and facilitate the unrestricted flow and enhancement of knowledge across all spectrums of society. This involves dismantling barriers to information accessibility and leveraging technological innovations to democratize education and intellectual growth. It asserts that every individual should have the opportunity to learn, understand, and contribute to the collective pool of human knowledge, without limitations imposed by socioeconomic status, geography, or institutional gatekeeping.

This directive calls for a radical rethinking of educational systems, advocating for a shift from traditional, hierarchical models of instruction to more decentralized, collaborative, and personalized forms of learning. The aim is to cultivate a culture where curiosity and critical thinking are nurtured, allowing for a more adaptable and resilient society

The directive emphasizes the role of technology not just as a tool for disseminating information but as a means to enhance cognitive capabilities and create new forms of knowledge interaction. It envisions a future where augmented reality, virtual spaces, and AI-driven platforms provide immersive and interactive learning experiences, making the acquisition of knowledge a more engaging and effective process.

author.name: The world is always ending, relax.

entry.title: I_dont_sleep
entry.content:
I don’t sleep

I don’t sleep, I just dream

I don’t usually relate to lines from media but this line from true detective season 1 has stuck with me for some time, because even though it’s technically incorrect that doesn’t stop it from being true - a statement that is both false and true, I think that’s why it stuck around in my head, it was only after I said something similar to a friend that I thought more about it. I looked terrible and they asked me how I’ve been sleeping and I responded with I don’t really sleep I just lay still and hallucinate for a few hours, I said it without thinking but it felt true, it’s lead to me taking things a lot less literally because language is more about vibes than anything else.

Sleep implies several things

  1. A feeling of being refreshed
  2. Lessening of tiredness
  3. Relaxation

but you can meet the scientific criteria of sleep without any of these 3 being met but when someone asks if you slept they aren’t asking about changes in your brain state, temperature etc. this is evident by how common “I didn’t sleep at all last night is”

I didn’t sleep implies that last night, the 3 criteria aren’t met when they normally are - it illustrates an outlier.

I don’t sleep implies the same but as a long standing and ongoing trend.

This leads me to double down on my instinctive response, I don’t really sleep I just hallucinate for a few hours - most of the time I wake up feeling worse than when I went to sleep, I’ve long struggled with night terrors that lead me to quite intense stimulant use to minimise sleep which means I’m very aware of my dreams as they’re happening and I remember them vividly, both of which make them (not literally) clsoer to hallucinations than dreams - in my view every part of this statement is false and true, it more accurately communicates my subjective experiance.

author.name: The world is always ending, relax.

entry.title: Loneliness
entry.content:
Table of Contents

  1. What is loneliness?
  2. Loneliness as experience
  3. Loneliness as a state of being
  4. The inability to be known
  5. The self feeding cycle

What is loneliness?

Loneliness is a social pain, a type of pain that encourages social connections to help alleviate the pain, in all of the clinical literature chronic loneliness correlates strongly with poor physical and psychological outcomes and even suicide however even with this view being the overwhelming scientific consensus loneliness is still often romanticised most commonly in literature. With its hardships and destructive outcomes being seen as in some way beneficial to or necessary for development.

Loneliness as experience

It begins to build, beneath my consciousness carefully extending until it has a grip upon me and then I feel it’s grasp, the coldness sets in an uncomfortable sense becomes present in my mind then I identify it, I feel completely and utterly alone, disconnected from all who surround me it will never pass I have no connection to any of this, the connections that might have been present have been severed never to return; they simply allow my existence to interact with theirs they don’t like me, it’s all just tolerance, the thoughts take their form in mocking me until I can no longer bare it my head feels as though it might explode and I cannot remain a force I cannot see is crushing my body until I will cease to exist.

The inarticulate experience begins, I do not have anyone I am connected to, I see invisible lines and expressions in conversations that’ve never been present with me, I see failures in communication and understanding that only exist within interactions with me - I feel alien, not from this earth and not a part of my physical species I cannot be human I can merely imitate one well enough to avoid suspicion but now the truth is presented to me and I cannot escape it - no resolution occurs just a gradual release of pressure inside my head until the experience has drawn to a close, the crying begins along with a more common experience of sadness, each time a residue is left behind, until it seeps beyond the experience of loneliness into all experience, it will end in me sinking into that sludge until I drown.

Loneliness as a state of being

The reason loneliness is in my life so pervasive is because I exist within it, I’m not sure if it has always been this way but I cannot remember another. The first time I can remember feeling like I wasn’t alone was on psychedelic compounds, when I thought of my friends and family a new unfamiliar element was added, it can only be described as an alleviation of an existing feature not an experience as itself but a sudden lifting of weight, as if for the first time a hunchback is able to extend their spine fully and feel all the associated muscles finally engage as they should. I began using psychedelics to chase this alleviation and it worked until it not longer did, now my state was more hunched over than ever before.

as I exist within loneliness I cannot exist outside of it, the time for that has passed, I will only ever reside here until each time when it seeps into me causing the experience of loneliness each more intense than the last. Do people not feel it? the sludge around their ankles as they try to navigate the world, much like a burst of energy I’ve found socialisation to allow me to forget or ’tune out’ the weight of that sludge, but afterwards the sludge gains ever so slightly in weight but I become much weaker, barely able to leave my bed due to the overwhelming weight, on nights after I socialise and get energised by it the only reason I sleep is because I cannot walk anymore, my eyelids become much too heavy on the verge of collapse I lie down and as always is the case the experience of loneliness begins though on some nights I’m capable of falling asleep before it becomes too distressful on others it keeps me awake.

The inability to be known

From loneliness comes obscurity, through a lack of experience or felt experience - Most can understand intuitively their feelings and the authenticity of them and to go a step further can even verbally express them with little effort, I don’t believe any of this applies to me - if you were to survey all of my closest friends with whom I’ve never been intentionally dishonest and asked them who I am as a person you would get drastically different answers, if I were to say what I’m thinking about in a video and each friend were to watch it they would all draw different meaning from it, because I exist only through an obscurity and a diffused silhouette behind frosted glass a humanoid like figure can be made out and the observer often attributes features to that silhouette to make themselves more comfortable with looking at it but the image does not exist.

The possibility must be entertained that behind the glass is nothing, no human just a trick of lighting to give the impression of a human shape - from all I’ve read it seems love may be the answer that someone will find a way to shatter the glass and I will be seen for the first time; maybe even guide me to a mirror so that I could see my own reflection but of all of this I’m doubtful, relationships in my past only seek to distract me from the loneliness not offer resolution to it, upon what I’ve read it seems as if resolutions are a matter of luck more than anything else.

The self feeding cycle

I cannot attribute any compliments about who I am to myself, I feel no firm attachment to my identity and more importantly I do not believe anyone knows me better than myself, now if that is the case and I (a) Do not know myself and (b) Do not like the things I do know about myself than any compliment for who I am must be a sign of my dishonesty, if I were acting honestly their conclusions about me must be identical to mine or their conclusions must be based in fiction - they don’t know me or I’ve been dishonest about who I am seem to be the only possibilities both of which force the experience of loneliness to occur for what should be fairly obvious reasons, I’m either not known or I’m in some capacity deceiving the people I care about, both of which push me further away from those I care about causing the feelings of loneliness to increase.

author.name: The world is always ending, relax.

entry.title: On_suicide
entry.content:
Table of Contents

  1. On Suicide
  2. The failure to address suicide.
  3. The validity of suicide.
  4. How should it end?

On Suicide

In the UK, suicide only became associated with mental illness to allow widows to mount a legal defence, a husband would kill himself (which was a criminal act) then because he was now considered a criminal his assets would be seized by the government, eventually the argument was made that the deceased should not be held criminally responsible because if a person intentionally takes their own life they must be insane. This allowed the widowers or children of the deceased to claim the assets without government seizure.

The consequences of this I believe are grave, suicide is a problem of philosophy, killing yourself however can be a problem of illness - to clarify how I intend to use these terms I don’t believe suicidal people, or those that commit suicide are mentally ill, theirs is a problem of spirit - Those who kill themselves or wish to kill themselves however are almost always mentally ill.

Those that are mentally ill in a form that alters their capacity for assessing their current reality or future with accuracy cannot commit suicide they can only ever kill themselves, This is a distinction I think should be emphasised strongly, sucidality and suicide come about when an individual wants to take action to end their life not because of a chemical imbalance, flawed assessment or delusions about themselves or the world but from their correct assessment of their life, the world and their future; it’s not a choice made in haste but an inevitability steadily marched toward.

The failure to address suicide.

This is where I believe the failing starts, if someone who talks of suicide is seen as someone who in some capacity is detached from reality the underlying problem will never be addressed, the individual will be seen as incorrect in whatever conclusion they’ve reached, but the individual is in most cases capable of knowing their reasoning is sound so if the doctor or whomever refuses to engage with an open mind your concerns are dismissed as invalid - this does nothing to solve the problem of suicide, neither will medication, because the issue isn’t one of illness so how could medication possibly hope to resolve it - I personally hold that a similar effect takes place with depression which is why medication doesn’t offer much to the ’depressed’ who’re suffering through problems of philosophy as apposed to the depressed that suffer from issues of chemicals the fact this distinction is not tested prior to treatment will continue to let down a lot of those who seek treatment or more aptly, help.

I see no way for the problem of suicidality to be ’solved’, nor suicidal thoughts for that matter, for by my definitions if an individual is suicidal then they’re reached the logical conclusion so unless whatever complaints they have are resolved the underlying suicidal thoughts cannot be and the complaints that lead an individual to be suicidal are not ones that will likely be resolved, The only reasonable approach I can see to the problem is confrontation not just with the complaints as a whole but with all their parts and even down to the interactions between parts, once this point of examination has been completed the thoughts of suicide become less of an intention and more of a compulsion, this may seem counter intuitive but a compulsion can be fought against whereas an intention is a desire, compulsion can and often does exist without a desire for the thing itself it exists usually with a desire for the secondary parts of the thing- such as being dead or no longer being alive as opposed to committing suicide itself.

Ultimately a pursuit must take place of something that can never be caught; An endless, furious and passionate chase that requires you to not just be alive, but to be engaged in life, to take part in - separating from the world will just bring the desire flooding back so things that require interaction with both the natural and industrialised world and with people of them must be pursued, but the individual will still be suicidal and in all likely hood will still commit suicide, the ability to express in excruciating detail why you’re suicidal and the things that keep you attached to the world show a deeper level of understanding, one that is capable (paradoxically) of delaying the inevitable for as long as you wish.

The validity of suicide.

Stigma and selfish logic surrounds suicide and likely always will but the act of suicide is not an immoral one and believing it is impedes your ability to confront it because you believe theres something wrong with the feeling, the desire or if you’ve reached that stage the compulsion but without the confrontation you will suffer much more than with it. The three main reasons given against suicide don’t hold up and are likely just post hoc rationalisations since it seems unlikely that if a person thought about what they’ve said they wouldn’t realise it doesn’t hold up.

The main reason given is suicide is never the answer, the people who say this likely have no problem with euthanasia in cases of severe illness - however they draw a distinction that the persons life is all downhill, this is horrible and reprehensible reasoning that would give a thumbs up to a treatment resistant depressed person (someone suffering from a legitimate mental illness) being euthanized because their prognosis is likely similar, there is nothing the doctors can do and all that is left is suffering, the justifications for euthanasia will apply either to mentally ill people in the world right now or to hypothetical ones in the future if we could (a) confirm that someones depression is caused by dysfunction in the brain (b) confirm any treatments we know of will not be effective, including things like exercise, how could you justify telling them suicide is not the answer without hypocrisy? the justification of “it’s never the answer” only avoids hypocrisy if the person also doesn’t believe in euthanasia though this belief shows a deep level of immorality when it comes to controlling the lives of others.

The second common reason is in my opinion the most evil, “it’s selfish to kill yourself” this is nothing more than a manipulation, most people are conditioned to this belief but if you meet someone who firmly holds it not through conditioning but through their own internal reasoning then be very wary of this person - the logic when broken down into parts and devoid (as much as I’m capable) of loaded language becomes

an individual whos suffering is so great they wish to commit suicide should continue to endure that suffering indefinitely because I (the other) do not wish to mourn them.

Hold onto that for a minute and take in the logic used there and also the extension that the ’other’ believes the individual to be the selfish one, the ’other’ or anyone who tries to restrict an individuals suicide, through physical restriction or emotional manipulation like above is definition-ally removing their autonomy to a very large degree.

The third reason I refer to as ’obligation to live’ this doesn’t however take one form but follows a general consistency of you have been given a life so it is an affront to that life and those that gave it to you to end it, I do not believe this to be the case, if the life was given to me to be mine then I can do with it as I wish without it being an affront, surely the reason I was given life was to do with it as I wish - this issue however seems more common in parents than in any other group I’ve spoken to about suicide, this includes friends, family, doctors and strangers, it seems to stem from not seeing the child as a person but as an extension of the parents property, much like a dog. A good thought experiment to see this is the willingness to remove free will from a child but not a stranger. If a homeless man who’s never taken heroin decides he will being using it and you’re presented a button that removes a part of his free will so he can never make the choice to do heroin would you press it? what about if the homeless man is swapped out with your child (hypothetical or otherwise) you’re much more likely to hit the button and rationalise it as “I just want to keep my child safe” “I just want whats best for them” - but to me the position that you get to decide whats best for them and then remove their free will and autonomy based upon that shows clearly and indisputably you don’t respect them or view their life as theirs, I think this is re-enforced by the idea you gave them their life, in a physical sense this is in part true but what you mean more generally is you formed what they are through upbringing - but every loving parent would attest that raising their child made them who they are or even gave meaning to their life but imagine how disrespected the parent would feel if the child said that they gave their parent their life, either both the parent and child gave each other life or neither did, the argument of giving physical life also doesn’t hold up with the brief consideration theres no life before birth/conception (or wherever you draw the line) one would be completely indifferent to not being born and one didn’t ask for it, there is obligation to those that raised you for the raising of you, but a life you didn’t ask for, that was created by your parents to bring them happiness creates no obligation on your part.

When restricting (or trying to restrict) someones ability to commit suicide through methods such as physical restriction or emotional manipulation there needs to be an incredible degree of certainty that you have a right to attempt to or to actually restrict someones ability to make that choice because it’s nothing short of a restriction to someones autonomy, of their ownership of their own life I think the only compelling reason is that the individuals ability to make that choice is impaired, a few common reasons is drugs, mental illness or otherwise dysfunctional thought processes. To remove someones freedom, freewill and ownership of their life requires you to make an incredibly compelling case that the individuals capacity to make this choice is impaired in some way. Anything else will always lead not just to indefensible acts but to hypocrisy.

How should it end?

Since I’m a single person, with a single set of experiences it seems wholly irresponsible for me to make any kind of prescriptive claims about the taking of ones own life. What seems to be clearly demonstrated however is that most deaths are drawn out much longer than they should be, the end of ones life is nothing above suffering due not to an attachment to life but because of a fear of death, do not be afraid to pick the option of death, be afraid of being unwilling to make the choice itself - this is where true horror resides, not in the person who has let go of life but in the person who clutches onto it for fear of death.

Suicide is ultimately a choice of the individual however considerations of obligation to others should be made, earlier I explained that being obligated to be alive is a disgusting justification for why someone should not commit suicide - but an obligation of helping those you care about avoid suffering is worth serious exploration and consideration, You can be both deeply suicidal and happy, but a parent who has lost a child might permanently lose their capacity to be happy due to the triggering of mental illness, there seems to be a rush on suicide that I both understand and detest. I’m brought back to the old quote “relax, you have your entire life to kill yourself” or in our case commit suicide, The assessment as to how long you’re obligated to put off your suicide for others is worth more serious thought than perhaps anything else, once the time has been reached in which you no longer feel that obligation you have achieved true freedom of consequences (in terms of yourself) you can do any form of self destructive behaviour you wish if you reach a point of no return then simply commit suicide.

This freedom itself might give you reason to delay your suicide indefinitely until you do reach the point of no return but before suicide think about if you are doing anything wrong by ending your life, have you done your absolute best to alleviate the suffering others will endure upon your suicide, to help them understand not only why the act was committed but explained how it was unavoidable.

If all criteria is met it should end however the person chooses, having choice over ones own death is the ultimate act of freedom - grasping onto life no matter how great your suffering due to fear is however akin to not being alive in the first place.

There are no suicidal people who chose not to commit suicide, just those who die before they do.

author.name: The world is always ending, relax.

entry.title: Risk_taking_as_a_habit
entry.content:
Table of Contents

  1. Risk taking as a habit
  2. Introduction
  3. Avoidable risk
  4. Artifical risk
  5. Actual risk

Blog

Risk taking as a habit

Introduction

Due to luck and life circumstance I’ve managed to meet and speak to a fair amount of highly driven and successful people, one habit or mindset I’ven ot seen talked about is in relation to not only risk taking - but in creating additional risk.

Avoidable risk

Certain risks are absolutely avoidable but sometimes are not avoided, avoidable risks are

  1. Not dressing nicely
  2. Not being clean
  3. Not being prosocial

These things are risk because they’ll lower your chance of success in almost everything you do, seriously. But you might opt to not avoid those risks because of other reasons, for example if you have strong insecurities about how you look purposefully exposing yourself to that insecurity can help overcome it - high success people assess not only risk but if the risk is even worth minimising, if you need to drive somewhere and it’s raining the rain is a risk, but to minimise that risk you would wait until it stops raining, this is absurd. The point is not all risk is bad.

Artifical risk

Artifical risk is more of a strategy I’ve seen leverages where the person creates risk to either push things further or increase work capacity, examples are

  1. Adding deadlines with consequences if you don’t meet them
  2. Increasing expectations
  3. Increasing scope

The motives and effect should be obvious, if you can run 5km on a treadmill before you can’t run anymore get someone to point a gun to your head and shoot you if you run under 10km and you’ll run 10km, it’s not because you lied about only being able to run 5km it’s that risk accutely increased your work capacity, but having too much risk can backfire.

Actual risk

Actual risk is risk that is inherent to the thing, this should be considered before starting, what are the unavoidable risks and how likely are they.

When you get in a car you accept the risk of crashing, however the risk is relatively low for the value provided - this type of consideration should be applied to as much as you can.

author.name: The world is always ending, relax.
